verdict_reasoning: HSPA1A is a canonical cytosolic Hsp70 chaperone with no membrane anchor in its baseline state. However, heat shock proteins including HSPA1A are well-documented to translocate to the cell surface during stress (heat shock, ER stress, oxidative stress) and in tumor cells as ecto-chaperones. Cancer cells display HSPA1A on the outer leaflet where it serves as a danger-associated molecular pattern and target for immunotherapy. Cell-stress-induced and cancer-cell-restricted ecto-presentation of this protein qualifies as contextual surface accessibility via cell_state_induced mechanism.
